sections.title: Overview
sections.content:
The **NetSuite** connector integrates Shielva with the [Oracle NetSuite SuiteTalk REST API](https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/netsuite/ns-online-help/section_1544431116.html) to sync customers, invoices, and items into the Shielva knowledge base and execute arbitrary SuiteQL queries.

NetSuite is a leading cloud ERP/CRM used by mid-market and enterprise companies worldwide. This connector exposes **9 API methods** across 4 resource domains — Customers, Invoices, Items, and SuiteQL — and implements a full sync engine that ingests all data as normalized `ConnectorDocument` objects.

**Key capabilities:**
- Token-Based Authentication (OAuth 1.0a, HMAC-SHA256) — no browser redirect needed
- Health check via `GET /record/v1/customer?limit=1`
- Full sync: Customers + Invoices + Items
- Paginated listing with `limit` and `offset` parameters
- Direct `get_customer` and `get_invoice` by NetSuite internal ID
- SuiteQL query engine for cross-record SQL-like queries
- Typed exception hierarchy (auth / rate-limit / not-found / network / server)
- Exponential backoff retry (auth errors never retried)
- All records normalized to `ConnectorDocument` with stable 16-char SHA-256 source IDs

sections.title: Quick Start
sections.content:
Get the NetSuite connector running in under 15 minutes.

### 1. Enable Required NetSuite Features

1. Navigate to **Setup > Company > Enable Features > SuiteCloud**
2. Enable **SuiteTalk REST Web Services**
3. Enable **Token-Based Authentication**
4. Save

### 2. Create an Integration Record

1. Go to **Setup > Integration > Integration Management > New**
2. Name it `Shielva Platform`, enable **Token-Based Authentication** under Authentication
3. Click Save — copy the **Consumer Key** and **Consumer Secret** (shown once only)

### 3. Create an Access Token

1. Go to **Setup > Users/Roles > Access Tokens > New**
2. Select your Integration, user, and role
3. Click Save — copy the **Token ID** and **Token Secret** (shown once only)

### 4. Install the Connector

In the Shielva ACP:
1. Navigate to **Integrations > NetSuite**
2. Enter your **Account ID**, **Consumer Key**, **Consumer Secret**, **Token Key**, and **Token Secret**
3. Click **Install** — Shielva verifies the credentials against your NetSuite instance

### 5. Run Your First Sync

```python
async with NetSuiteConnector(
    tenant_id="tenant-001",
    connector_id="netsuite-001",
    config={
        "account_id": "1234567",
        "consumer_key": "YOUR_CONSUMER_KEY",
        "consumer_secret": "YOUR_CONSUMER_SECRET",
        "token_key": "YOUR_TOKEN_KEY",
        "token_secret": "YOUR_TOKEN_SECRET",
    }
) as conn:
    result = await conn.sync(full=True)
    print(f"Synced {result.documents_synced} records")
```

sections.title: Authentication
sections.content:
The NetSuite connector uses **Token-Based Authentication (TBA)** — NetSuite's implementation of OAuth 1.0a with HMAC-SHA256 signatures.

### Credential Fields

| Field | Key | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|-----|------|----------|-------------|
| Account ID | `account_id` | string | Yes | NetSuite account ID (e.g. `1234567` or `1234567_SB1` for sandbox) |
| Consumer Key | `consumer_key` | string | Yes | From your NetSuite Integration record |
| Consumer Secret | `consumer_secret` | password | Yes | From your NetSuite Integration record — shown once at creation |
| Token Key | `token_key` | string | Yes | Token ID from Setup > Users/Roles > Access Tokens |
| Token Secret | `token_secret` | password | Yes | Token Secret — shown once at creation |

### How TBA Signing Works

Every HTTP request to the NetSuite REST API is signed on-the-fly with a fresh OAuth 1.0a Authorization header:

1. Collect OAuth params: `consumer_key`, `token`, `signature_method=HMAC-SHA256`, `timestamp`, `nonce`, `version=1.0`
2. Build a percent-encoded parameter string (sorted lexicographically)
3. Build the signature base string: `METHOD & URL & PARAMS` (all percent-encoded)
4. Sign with `HMAC-SHA256(consumer_secret & token_secret, base_string)`
5. Base64-encode the digest and append as `oauth_signature`
6. Send as `Authorization: OAuth realm="...", oauth_consumer_key="...", ...`

This happens per-request with a fresh nonce and timestamp — no token caching or refresh is needed.

### Security

- All five credential values are stored AES-256-GCM encrypted in the Shielva vault
- Credentials are never logged or written to disk
- The Consumer Secret and Token Secret are write-only after first save — they cannot be retrieved from Shielva's UI
- All communication uses TLS 1.2+

sections.title: Configuration
sections.content:
Every `install_field` listed below is collected from the user **once** at install time and stored in the connector's encrypted config. `config_keys` are values the connector derives at runtime (e.g. tokens) and persists for subsequent calls.

### Install fields

| Key | Label | Type | Required | Description |
|-----|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `account_id` | Account ID | `string` | ✅ | Your NetSuite account ID. Find it under Setup > Company > Company Information > NetSuite Account ID. For sandbox accounts append _SB1 (e.g. 1234567_SB1). |
| `consumer_key` | Consumer Key | `string` | ✅ | The Consumer Key generated when you created your Integration record in NetSuite (Setup > Integration > Integration Management). |
| `consumer_secret` | Consumer Secret | `password` | ✅ | The Consumer Secret from your NetSuite Integration record. Shown only once at creation — store it securely. |
| `token_key` | Token Key | `string` | ✅ | The Token ID generated under Setup > Users/Roles > Access Tokens > New or via the User Provisioning page. |
| `token_secret` | Token Secret | `password` | ✅ | The Token Secret corresponding to your Token ID. Shown only once at creation. |

### Runtime config keys

_No runtime-derived config keys for this connector._

### Defaults & validation

- All values are validated on `initialize()` — missing required fields raise `MissingConfigError`, malformed values raise `InvalidConfigError`.
- Numeric and boolean fields are coerced from strings (e.g. `"true"` → `True`) so configuration loaded from environment variables is accepted unchanged.
- Secrets are never echoed back in responses or logs; only field keys appear in audit entries.

sections.title: API Methods
sections.content: The connector exposes 9 public async methods.

children.title: install()
children.content:
Validates all five TBA credential fields are present and non-empty, then verifies them by calling `GET /record/v1/customer?limit=1`.

```python
result = await connector.install()
# InstallResult(health=HEALTHY, auth_status=CONNECTED, connector_id='1234567', message='Connected to NetSuite account 1234567...')
```

**Returns:** `InstallResult` with `health`, `auth_status`, `connector_id`, `message`

**Error cases:**
- Any missing field → `OFFLINE / MISSING_CREDENTIALS` (lists which fields are missing)
- Auth failure (401/403) → `OFFLINE / INVALID_CREDENTIALS`
- Network failure → `OFFLINE / FAILED`

children.title: health_check()
children.content:
Verifies connectivity by calling `GET /record/v1/customer?limit=1`.

```python
result = await connector.health_check()
# HealthCheckResult(health=HEALTHY, auth_status=CONNECTED, message='NetSuite REST API reachable — account 1234567')
```

**Returns:** `HealthCheckResult` with `health`, `auth_status`, `message`

**Health states:**
- `HEALTHY / CONNECTED` — API responded successfully
- `OFFLINE / MISSING_CREDENTIALS` — one or more fields are empty
- `OFFLINE / INVALID_CREDENTIALS` — 401 or 403 from NetSuite
- `DEGRADED / FAILED` — network timeout or 5xx error

children.title: sync(full, since, kb_id)
children.content:
Syncs NetSuite customers, invoices, and items into the Shielva knowledge base.

```python
# Full sync — up to 100 records per entity type
result = await connector.sync(full=True, kb_id="kb_netsuite")

print(result.documents_found)   # total records fetched
print(result.documents_synced)  # successfully normalized + ingested
print(result.documents_failed)  # failed normalizations
print(result.status)            # COMPLETED / PARTIAL / FAILED
```

**Sync order and failure modes:**
1. Fetch customers → failure returns `FAILED` (stops here)
2. Fetch invoices → failure returns `PARTIAL` (customers already synced)
3. Fetch items → failure returns `PARTIAL` (customers + invoices already synced)

**Status definitions:**
- `COMPLETED` — all entities fetched and normalized successfully
- `PARTIAL` — some entity fetch or normalization failed
- `FAILED` — customer fetch failed (no data synced)

children.title: list_customers(limit, offset)
children.content:
Returns a paginated list of NetSuite customer records.

```python
# First page
page = await connector.list_customers(limit=100, offset=0)
# {"count": 100, "hasMore": true, "items": [{"id": "42", "companyName": "Acme Corp", ...}]}

# Second page
page2 = await connector.list_customers(limit=100, offset=100)
```

**NetSuite endpoint:** `GET /record/v1/customer`

**Response shape:**
```json
{
  "count": 100,
  "hasMore": true,
  "offset": 0,
  "totalResults": 450,
  "items": [{...}]
}
```

children.title: get_customer(customer_id)
children.content:
Returns a single NetSuite customer record by internal ID.

```python
customer = await connector.get_customer("42")
# {"id": "42", "companyName": "Acme Corporation", "email": "billing@acme.com", ...}
```

**NetSuite endpoint:** `GET /record/v1/customer/{customer_id}`

**Raises:** `NetSuiteNotFoundError` if the customer does not exist

children.title: list_invoices(limit, offset)
children.content:
Returns a paginated list of NetSuite invoice (transaction) records.

```python
page = await connector.list_invoices(limit=100, offset=0)
# {"count": 50, "hasMore": false, "items": [{"id": "100", "tranId": "INV-1001", ...}]}
```

**NetSuite endpoint:** `GET /record/v1/invoice`

children.title: get_invoice(invoice_id)
children.content:
Returns a single NetSuite invoice by internal ID.

```python
invoice = await connector.get_invoice("100")
# {"id": "100", "tranId": "INV-1001", "total": 5000.00, ...}
```

**NetSuite endpoint:** `GET /record/v1/invoice/{invoice_id}`

**Raises:** `NetSuiteNotFoundError` if the invoice does not exist

children.title: list_items(limit, offset)
children.content:
Returns a paginated list of NetSuite item records (all item types: inventory, service, non-inventory, etc.).

```python
page = await connector.list_items(limit=100, offset=0)
# {"count": 200, "hasMore": true, "items": [{"id": "200", "displayName": "Professional Services", ...}]}
```

**NetSuite endpoint:** `GET /record/v1/item`

children.title: suiteql(query, limit, offset)
children.content:
Execute an arbitrary SuiteQL query against the NetSuite query engine.

SuiteQL is NetSuite's SQL-like language that supports JOINs, aggregations, and custom field access across all record types.

```python
# Get active customers with their balance
result = await connector.suiteql(
    query="SELECT id, companyName, email, balance FROM customer WHERE isInactive = 'F' ORDER BY companyName",
    limit=1000,
    offset=0
)
for row in result["items"]:
    print(row["companyname"], row["balance"])

# Get open invoices from the last 30 days
result = await connector.suiteql(
    query="SELECT t.id, t.tranid, t.entity, t.amount, t.duedate FROM transaction t WHERE t.type = 'CustInvc' AND t.status = 'A' AND t.trandate >= BUILTIN.RELATIVE_RANGES('LM', 'START')"
)
```

**NetSuite endpoint:** `POST /query/v1/suiteql`

**Required permission:** SuiteAnalytics Workbook (View) on the role

**Response shape:**
```json
{
  "count": 2,
  "hasMore": false,
  "items": [
    {"id": "42", "companyname": "Acme Corp", "balance": "2500.00"},
    {"id": "43", "companyname": "Globex Inc", "balance": "0.00"}
  ]
}
```

**Note:** SuiteQL column names in the response are always lowercase regardless of how they appear in the query.

sections.title: Data Model
sections.content:
All records are normalized into `ConnectorDocument` objects before being ingested into the Shielva knowledge base.

### ConnectorDocument

```python
@dataclass
class ConnectorDocument:
    source_id: str        # SHA-256(entity_type:internal_id)[:16]
    title: str            # Human-readable label
    content: str          # Newline-separated key-value text
    connector_id: str     # Shielva connector instance ID
    tenant_id: str        # Shielva tenant ID
    source_url: str       # NetSuite deep-link URL (if available)
    metadata: dict        # Entity-specific structured fields
```

### Customer Document

```json
{
  "source_id": "a3f2b1c4d5e6f7a8",
  "title": "Customer: Acme Corporation",
  "content": "Customer: Acme Corporation\nEntity ID: CUST-42\nEmail: billing@acme.com\nPhone: 555-1234\nBalance: 2500.0\nActive: True\nSubsidiary: Parent Company",
  "metadata": {
    "entity_type": "customer",
    "internal_id": "42",
    "entity_id": "CUST-42",
    "company_name": "Acme Corporation",
    "email": "billing@acme.com",
    "phone": "555-1234",
    "balance": 2500.0,
    "is_inactive": false,
    "subsidiary": "Parent Company"
  }
}
```

### Invoice Document

```json
{
  "source_id": "b8c9d0e1f2a3b4c5",
  "title": "Invoice #INV-1001",
  "metadata": {
    "entity_type": "invoice",
    "internal_id": "100",
    "tran_id": "INV-1001",
    "customer_name": "Acme Corporation",
    "total": 5000.0,
    "amount_remaining": 5000.0,
    "tran_date": "2026-01-15",
    "due_date": "2026-02-15",
    "status": "Open",
    "subsidiary": "Parent Company"
  }
}
```

### Item Document

```json
{
  "source_id": "c1d2e3f4a5b6c7d8",
  "title": "Item: Professional Services",
  "metadata": {
    "entity_type": "item",
    "internal_id": "200",
    "item_id": "ITEM-200",
    "display_name": "Professional Services",
    "description": "Consulting services billed per hour",
    "sales_price": 150.0,
    "item_type": "Service",
    "is_inactive": false
  }
}
```

### Stable ID Generation

Source IDs are computed as:
```python
import hashlib
stable_id = hashlib.sha256(f"{entity_type}:{internal_id}".encode()).hexdigest()[:16]
```

This ensures documents are de-duplicated correctly across incremental syncs.

sections.title: Sync Behavior
sections.content:
`sync()` is the bulk ingestion entry point. A single call pulls every resource this connector knows how to sync from NetSuite, normalizes each one into a `ConnectorDocument`, and returns a `SyncResult` summarizing what was processed.

### Sync lifecycle

1. **Listing** — the connector requests the first page of each resource type from the provider's list endpoint.
2. **Pagination** — subsequent pages are fetched until the provider signals end-of-stream (cursor exhaustion, `has_more=false`, or empty page).
3. **Normalization** — each raw record is passed through the resource-specific `normalize_*` helper, which extracts fields, computes the `source_id`, and emits a `ConnectorDocument`.
4. **Deduplication** — when the same resource appears across pages (rare, but possible during eventual-consistency windows), the deterministic `source_id` ensures the downstream knowledge base treats it as a single record, not a duplicate.
5. **Partial-failure tolerance** — a per-resource failure is logged and the sync continues with the next resource. The returned `SyncResult` exposes `status=PARTIAL` and an `errors` list when this happens.

### Pagination strategy

- **Cursor-based** providers (most modern APIs) — the connector tracks the `next_cursor` field on each page and stops when the provider omits it.
- **Offset/limit** providers — the connector requests fixed-size pages and stops when a page returns fewer records than `limit`.
- **Token-based (e.g. `next_page_token`)** — same as cursor-based but the token is opaque and forwarded verbatim.

### Rate-limit handling

When the provider returns `429 Too Many Requests`, the connector honors the `Retry-After` header if present, otherwise applies exponential backoff (1s, 2s, 4s, 8s, 16s capped at 30s) with full jitter. The retry budget per request is bounded so a misbehaving provider cannot stall the sync indefinitely.

### Incremental sync

The connector treats every `sync()` as a full re-walk of the listing endpoints. Idempotency is achieved via stable `source_id`s rather than incremental cursors. If the provider exposes a `since` / `updated_after` parameter, the connector uses the last successful sync timestamp; otherwise it pulls the full list and lets deduplication handle overlap.

### Features enabled

This connector is configured with: error_handling, hmac_sha256, normalizer, oauth1_tba, rate_limiting, retry, suiteql.

sections.title: Error Handling
sections.content:
Every failure mode this connector encounters maps to a typed exception. Catching the base class — `NetSuiteError` — handles every connector-raised error in one block; catching a specific subclass lets you react to a particular failure mode.

### Exception hierarchy

```
NetSuiteError(Exception)
  NetSuiteAuthError(NetSuiteError)
  NetSuiteRateLimitError(NetSuiteError)
  NetSuiteNotFoundError(NetSuiteError)
  NetSuiteNetworkError(NetSuiteError)
  NetSuiteServerError(NetSuiteError)
  NetSuiteValidationError(NetSuiteError)
```

### When each is raised

- **`NetSuiteError`** — Base exception for all NetSuite connector errors.
- **`NetSuiteAuthError`** — Raised when NetSuite rejects credentials or the OAuth 1.0a signature (401/403).
- **`NetSuiteRateLimitError`** — Raised on 429 Too Many Requests from the NetSuite REST API.
- **`NetSuiteNotFoundError`** — Raised when a requested NetSuite resource does not exist (404).
- **`NetSuiteNetworkError`** — Raised on transient network failures (timeouts, connection errors).
- **`NetSuiteServerError`** — Raised on 5xx responses from the NetSuite REST API.
- **`NetSuiteValidationError`** — Raised when install_fields are missing or malformed before a request is sent.

### Retry policy

- **Transient HTTP errors** (network resets, `502`, `503`, `504`) — retried up to 3 times with exponential backoff + jitter.
- **Rate-limit responses** (`429`) — retry honoring `Retry-After`, capped at 5 attempts.
- **Authentication errors** (`401`, `403`) — not retried; surface immediately so the caller can re-authorize.
- **4xx client errors** (`400`, `404`, `422`) — not retried; raised as a typed exception so the caller can fix the request.

### Circuit breaker

A per-connector circuit breaker opens after 5 consecutive failures within a 30-second window and stays open for 30 seconds. While open, every method call raises `CircuitBreakerOpen` immediately instead of incurring the network round-trip — protecting the provider from a thundering-herd retry storm when it is genuinely down.

sections.title: Testing
sections.content:
Every connector ships with a unit test suite under `tests/`. Unit tests mock the HTTP layer so they run offline; integration tests (if configured) hit the live provider API and require valid credentials in the environment.

### Running unit tests

```bash
cd netsuite_connector
python -m pytest -q tests/
```

### Test layout

- `tests/test_connector.py` — the primary suite. One `test_*` function per public connector method, covering the happy path, the empty-result path, and the error path for each.
- `tests/__init__.py` — package marker; no shared fixtures live here.
- HTTP responses are mocked with `httpx.MockTransport` or `pytest-httpx` so the tests are deterministic and fast.

### Test coverage at a glance

This connector ships **74** test functions. The suite covers authentication, pagination, normalization, the connector's main API methods, and the error paths for each one.

### Integration tests

Integration tests are gated by the presence of credentials in the environment (e.g. `RUN_INTEGRATION=1` plus the connector's install-field values prefixed with `NETSUITE_`). When the gate is absent, the suite short-circuits with a `pytest.skip` so CI doesn't fail in environments without live access.

sections.title: Troubleshooting
sections.content:
| Symptom | Likely cause | Fix |
|---|---|---|
| `401 Unauthorized` | Invalid Consumer Key, Consumer Secret, Token Key, or Token Secret | Re-copy all four credential values from NetSuite — ensure you are using the correct environment (prod vs sandbox) |
| `403 Forbidden` | Role lacks required permissions | Add View access for Customers, Transactions, Items, and REST Web Services to the role |
| `Invalid consumer key` in `o:errorDetails` | Consumer Key does not match any Integration record | Verify the Integration record is enabled and the Consumer Key is copied correctly |
| `Invalid signature` | Consumer Secret or Token Secret is wrong | Re-copy the secrets; if lost, revoke and regenerate credentials |
| Connector shows MISSING_CREDENTIALS | One or more fields is empty | The error message lists which fields are missing — fill them all in |
| Account hostname not resolving | Wrong Account ID or missing sandbox suffix | Check Setup > Company > Company Information; append `_SB1` for sandbox |
| `SuiteTalk REST Web Services not enabled` | Feature disabled | Setup > Company > Enable Features > SuiteCloud — enable SuiteTalk REST Web Services |
| TBA disabled | Feature disabled | Setup > Company > Enable Features > SuiteCloud — enable Token-Based Authentication |
| SuiteQL returns 403 | Missing SuiteAnalytics Workbook permission | Add SuiteAnalytics Workbook (View) to the role, or switch to direct record REST endpoints |
| Rate limit errors | NetSuite API concurrency limits | Connector retries automatically; reduce sync frequency for persistent throttling |

sections.title: FAQ
sections.content:
### Does this connector support multiple accounts of the same provider?

Yes. Each NetSuite account is a separate connector *instance* with its own `connector_id`, install fields, and encrypted credentials. The provider key in the catalog is shared, but the multi-tenant data layer keeps the instances strictly isolated.

### Can I sync only some resource types?

Yes. The connector exposes a `sync_resources` config option (a list of resource type strings). When set, `sync()` walks only the listed types and skips the rest. Leave it unset to sync everything the connector knows about.

### How does deletion propagate?

On each `sync()`, the connector compares the set of `source_id`s returned by NetSuite against the set it last persisted to the knowledge base. IDs that are absent on the new walk are deleted from the knowledge base, so deletions in NetSuite propagate to Shielva on the next sync.

### What happens during a rate-limit hit?

The connector inspects `Retry-After` and waits the indicated interval. If the header is absent, it falls back to exponential backoff (1s, 2s, 4s, 8s, 16s) with full jitter, retrying up to 5 times. After that it raises a `RateLimitError` so the caller can decide whether to back off further.

### Are credentials encrypted at rest?

Yes — AES-256-GCM with a per-tenant data encryption key derived via HKDF-SHA256 from the master key. Plaintext exists in memory only for the lifetime of an in-flight request; the secret never appears in logs, audit entries, or telemetry.

### How do I add a new method?

1. Add the method to the `NetSuiteConnector` class in `connector.py`.
2. Add a corresponding `test_<method>` to `tests/test_connector.py` covering happy/empty/error paths.
3. Re-run the documentation generator — the new method will appear in **API Methods** automatically.

### Can the connector run in a worker / serverless context?

Yes. The connector is fully `async` and holds no global state, so it is safe to instantiate per-request (or per-task) inside Celery, AWS Lambda, or a worker pool. For long-running processes, instantiate once and reuse — the underlying HTTP client maintains a keep-alive connection pool.

sections.title: Changelog
sections.content:
All notable changes to the **NetSuite connector** are recorded here. This project follows [Semantic Versioning](https://semver.org/): `MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH` where `MAJOR` is incremented for breaking interface changes, `MINOR` for backwards-compatible features, and `PATCH` for bug fixes.

## [1.0.0] — Current

### Added
- Initial release of the NetSuite connector.
- Full sync of all primary resource types with normalization into `ConnectorDocument`.
- Public connector methods for direct API access (see **API Methods**).
- Typed exception hierarchy with retry-aware classification (see **Error Handling**).
- Health check endpoint for fast credential validation.

### Stability
- Multi-tenant safe — every query is scoped by `tenant_id`.
- Idempotent ingestion — deterministic `source_id` ensures resyncs don't duplicate.
- Circuit breaker protects upstream during sustained outage.

### Upgrade notes
- This is the initial release; no upgrade path is required.
- Future PATCH releases are guaranteed not to break existing config or method signatures.
- MINOR releases may introduce new optional methods or features; existing call sites continue to work unchanged.
- MAJOR releases will be accompanied by a migration guide in this section.
